cb_layout: c0_input(2), c1_scaler(1), c2_mm_scaler(1), c3_max(2), c4_exp_sum(2), c5_recip_sum(2), c16_output(2)

description: Reader reads tiles from DRAM, compute copies c0->c16, writer writes c16 to DRAM. Identity passthrough.

approach: Single pass tile reader using TensorAccessor. Generates scaler tiles c1 and c2 at startup. Reads all NC*Ht*Wt tiles one at a time into c0.

change: Added num_rows_or_cols as reader CT arg index 2
reason: Reader needs total row/col count to iterate all NC*Ht*Wt tiles, not just Ht*Wt

approach: Updated to use num_rows_or_cols CT arg (index 2) and TensorAccessorArgs<3>. Reads num_rows_or_cols*Wt tiles total in row-major order.

change: Added num_tiles as reader CT arg index 3
reason: Reader needs total tile count directly since num_rows_or_cols*Wt is only correct for dim=-1, not dim=-2

approach: Uses num_tiles CT arg (index 3) directly for total tile count. TensorAccessorArgs<4>. Simple sequential read loop.
Simple copy_tile passthrough: cb_input(c0) -> DST -> cb_output(c16). Uses compute_kernel_hw_startup, copy_tile_init, tile_regs lifecycle.
Sequential tile writer: cb_wait_front(c16,1), noc_async_write via TensorAccessor, cb_pop_front. TensorAccessorArgs<1>.

cb_summary: c0: reader push 1/compute wait 1 pop 1. c1: push 1/unused. c2: push 1/unused. c16: compute push 1/writer wait 1 pop 1. All balanced.

upstream_fixes: Added num_rows_or_cols and num_tiles as reader CT args

description: Compute applies exp_tile to each tile. Reader/writer unchanged from stage 1.

approach: Added exp_tile<false>(0) after copy_tile. Added exp.h include and exp_tile_init<false>() at startup.

cb_summary: Same as data_pipeline. c0: push1/wait1/pop1. c16: reserve1/push1/wait1/pop1. Only compute logic changed (added exp_tile).

description: Wrong include path: api/compute/eltwise_unary/sfpu/exp.h should be api/compute/eltwise_unary/exp.h

change: Fixed include path from api/compute/eltwise_unary/sfpu/exp.h to api/compute/eltwise_unary/exp.h

design_deviations: Fixed include path from api/compute/eltwise_unary/sfpu/exp.h to api/compute/eltwise_unary/exp.h

description: Full softmax for dim=-1. 3-pass reader, 6-phase compute (max, reduce_max, sub+exp+sum, reduce_sum+recip, final_mul), writer. Complete rewrite of all 3 kernels.

approach: 3-pass reader with DIM_W/DIM_H ifdef. For dim=-1: iterates rows, reads Wt tiles 3x per row. For dim=-2: iterates columns, reads Ht tiles 3x per column in column-major order.

change: Added DIM_W/DIM_H defines to reader kernel descriptor
reason: Reader uses ifdef DIM_W/DIM_H to select row-major vs column-major 3-pass tile ordering

approach: 3-phase per row: Phase1 reduce<MAX,REDUCE_ROW> helper -> c3. Phase2 sub_bcast+exp+matmul_tiles sum accum+recip -> c5. Phase3 sub_bcast+exp+mul_bcast -> c16. DIM_W/DIM_H ifdef for COL vs ROW broadcast.

Fixed Phase 3: pack exp to c4 temp, then mul_bcast from c4*c5 into DST, pack to c16. Phase 2: sub_bcast+exp->c4->matmul_tiles accum in DST[0]+recip->c5. mm_init called per matmul tile for reinit after bcast.

cb_summary: c0: 3*Wt push/3*Wt pop per row. c1: push1/persist. c2: push1/persist. c3: push1(reduce)/pop1(end phase3). c4: push1/pop1 per tile in phase2+3. c5: push1(end phase2)/pop1(end phase3). c16: push1/pop1 per tile phase3 only.

details: 1x1x32x32 PASSED, 1x1x64x128 FAILED with max diff 0.509765625. Single-tile case works but multi-tile fails.

description: DST accumulator (DST[0]) for matmul sum is lost between tiles because tile_regs_release/acquire cycle clears DST. Need to keep DST[0] alive across inner_dim loop by using acquire_dst/release_dst or avoiding release between tiles.
evidence: Single tile (Wt=1) passes, multi-tile (Wt=4) fails with large diff - accumulation not working

detail: Phase 2 restructured to use acquire_dst/release_dst full-sync to keep DST[0] matmul accumulator alive across inner_dim loop. Phase 3 uses half-sync tile_regs for per-tile exp+mul_bcast.

summary: Phase1: reduce helper manages cb_input consume and cb_max produce internally. Phase2: reader pushes cb_input 1 tile at a time, compute waits/pops cb_input 1 at a time; compute produces cb_exp_sum 1 tile, then waits/pops cb_exp_sum 1 for matmul; compute produces cb_recip_sum 1 tile. Phase3: reader pushes cb_input 1 at a time, compute waits/pops 1; compute produces/consumes cb_exp_sum 1; compute waits cb_recip_sum 1 (produced in P2), produces cb_output 1. Writer consumes cb_output 1 at a time. Persistent: cb_scaler(1) and cb_mm_scaler(1) pushed once by reader, never popped. All push/pop balanced per iteration.

detail: Device timeout on first shape 1x1x32x32. Hang detected during readback (to_torch). Need to check triage output.

Phase 2 hangs because pack_tile(1, cb_exp_sum) and pack_tile(0, cb_recip_sum) are called inside acquire_dst/release_dst block WITHOUT tile_regs_commit/tile_regs_wait around them. In full-sync mode, acquire_dst holds DST but pack_tile still needs commit/wait to hand control to packer. Missing commit/wait means packer never sees data, causing TRISC1 (math) to deadlock. Fix: add tile_regs_commit+tile_regs_wait before and after each pack_tile call within the acquire_dst block.

REVISED: acquire_dst deadlock confirmed. PACK thread blocks in acquire_dst because no unconsumed math_done signal exists after reduce helper last cycle. NEW APPROACH: Replace matmul-based sum accumulation with reduce<SUM, REDUCE_ROW> helper + recip_tile post_reduce_op. Phase 2 becomes: (a) sub+exp+pack to cb_exp_sum for all inner_dim tiles, (b) reduce<SUM> on cb_exp_sum -> cb_recip_sum. Requires increasing cb_exp_sum to inner_dim tiles. This avoids DST accumulator management entirely.

change: Increased cb_exp_sum from 2 tiles to inner_dim tiles
reason: Phase 2 needs to buffer all inner_dim exp tiles before reduce<SUM> consumes them. Replacing matmul-based accumulation with reduce helper approach.

approach: Rewrote Phase 2 to use reduce<SUM, REDUCE_DIM> helper with recip_tile post_reduce_op instead of matmul-based accumulation. Phase 2a: sub+exp+pack all inner_dim tiles to cb_exp. Phase 2b: reduce<SUM> consumes cb_exp, produces 1 recip_sum tile. Removed cb_mm_scaler dependency. Fixed DIM_H bcast inits to use sub_bcast_rows_init_short and mul_bcast_rows_init_short.

summary: Phase1: reduce helper manages cb_input(inner_dim) and cb_max(1). Phase2a: compute waits/pops cb_input 1 at a time, pushes to cb_exp 1 at a time (inner_dim total). Phase2b: reduce<SUM> helper waits/pops cb_exp per tile (inner_dim), produces cb_recip_sum(1). Phase3: compute waits/pops cb_input 1 at a time, produces/consumes cb_exp 1 at a time, produces cb_output 1 at a time. Writer consumes cb_output 1 at a time. Reader pushes 3*inner_dim per row matching 3 phases. cb_scaler persistent. All balanced.

detail: Python UnboundLocalError: inner_dim referenced before assignment in program descriptor. CB descriptor for cb_exp_sum uses inner_dim but work distribution section where inner_dim is computed comes after CB section. Free fix - code ordering issue.
inner_dim is computed in section 4 (WORK DISTRIBUTION) but used in section 3 (CB DESCRIPTORS) for cb_exp_sum total_size. Fix: move work distribution section before CB descriptors.

change: Moved work distribution section (inner_dim computation) before CB descriptors section; removed duplicate
reason: cb_exp_sum total_size uses inner_dim which must be computed first

detail: Moved work distribution section before CB descriptors to fix UnboundLocalError on inner_dim. Free fix - Python ordering issue.
All 4 shapes passed: 1x1x32x32, 1x1x64x128, 1x1x32x256, 2x3x64x64. Full softmax dim=-1 working with reduce<SUM> approach.

upstream_fixes: work distribution moved before CB descriptors
